source: vidIQ Instagram/TikTok outlier search (live connector), 1 bounded call = 5 credits
query: aphantasia — cannot picture an apple, blind mind's eye, mental imagery self-test

directTopicMatch.note: APHANTASIA ITSELF travels: a 121s explainer that walks the viewer through the visualization test hit 26.5x NPR's own median. Confirms topic demand independent of format.

patternA_selfTest_participation.claim: Viewer self-test / 'what do you see' participation formats dominate this niche's outliers.

instances.concept: can you follow the red ball — attention test
which animal do you see first — personality test
zebra or lion — what do you see first
what do you see — brain dominance

patternA_selfTest_participation.note: 5 of 10 returned outliers are viewer-self-tests, all 32-58x. This CONFIRMS the playbook's existing CONFIRMED rule (participation beats passive explanation) on fresh external data. Aphantasia's apple test is natively this format.

patternB_faceless_animated_education.claim: Faceless motion-graphic education about a perception/brain fact scales — and does NOT require dense scene changes.

instances.concept: your brain filters out 99.99% of reality — animated diagram
instances.execution: pacing SLOW, static text, visual_changes LOW, music only, looped

patternB_faceless_animated_education.note: IMPORTANT and partly CONTRARY to our recent working assumption. The single highest outlier in the whole sweep (490x, 1.4M views, 40K-follower creator) is SLOW-paced, LOW visual-change, static-text, looped, faceless animation — i.e. close to what we already build. It suggests our MICROGRAVITY problem was not 'too few scenes' per se but a weak participation hook. n=1 on the 490x; do NOT promote.

_discipline: CANDIDATE / external evidence only. Per CLAUDE.md and AUTOPILOT_REVIEW_PROTOCOL, external outliers guide topic and packaging hypotheses but NEVER become Curio rules until our own platform-separated posted analytics support them. Do not write to approved-patterns.json or the rules DB from this file.
